tests.intent: The test adapter consumes the approved DOOM_R1_PIXELS session-bound interface and independently observes all required frame fields.
Spawn east, spawn north, and spawn south are rendered from bound player rows with the fixed eye height and exact headings.
Each SQL observation contains exactly one palette index for every ordered column and row key in the 320 by 200 canvas.
Every database-produced palette index is an integer from zero through 255 before any client-side transformation.
SQL-derived column runs are positive, adjacent, maximally merged, column-local, and cover rows zero through 199 exactly.
Expanding canonical SQL runs equals all 64000 SQL pixels in both relational MINUS directions for every pose.
The test-only decoder expands the SQL-derived RLE to the exact ordered palette index at all 64000 positions for all poses.
Independent palette lookup yields exact red, green, blue, and opaque alpha bytes for every decoded pixel in all poses.
Each artifact is an 8-bit indexed PNG with fixed dimensions, one complete palette, and no ancillary or nondeterministic chunks.
Independently parsing each PNG returns the exact palette table and all ordered palette indices from the SQL frame.
Encoding identical pixels and palette twice produces byte-identical PNG data and stable SHA-256 diagnostic identities.
Six named columns per pose report canonical run tuples, per-column palette-byte hashes, and complete row coverage.
Eight named pixel positions per pose report SQL palette index and independently expanded RGBA values for review.
The three diagnostic poses retain distinct SQL frame and PNG identities rather than replaying one embedded output.
Visible R1 golden hashes remain absent and explicitly pending until the user reviews the generated PNG artifacts.
The test-only decoder has no production dependency, fixture-specific frame bytes, expected visible hashes, network reads, or shell escape.
Production sources cannot import evaluator code, read evaluator or golden paths, emit evaluator PASS text, or embed diagnostic answers.
Isolated semantic mutations of ordering, RLE, palette, RGBA, PNG structure, pose binding, and golden policy are all detected.
